message:
fix(review-r4): tighten launchd ThrottleInterval plist assertion

The regression test from 28bb678c split its check into two independent
plist.contains() assertions:

  assert!(plist.contains("<key>ThrottleInterval</key>"));
  assert!(plist.contains("<integer>30</integer>"));

Both would still pass even if ThrottleInterval silently regressed back to
launchd's 10s default, as long as some OTHER `<integer>30</integer>`
survived somewhere in the plist — a future StartInterval, a KeepAlive
retry count, or anything else that happens to serialize to 30. The test
would then give a false "still capped at 30s" verdict while production
quietly floods ~/Library/Logs/agent-mail/ at the 10s rate.

Replace with a single substring check covering the adjacent key/value
pair:

  plist.contains("<key>ThrottleInterval</key>\n    <integer>30</integer>")

Same format! layout, exact indentation, one guaranteed pairing. A
regression that changes either the key name, the value, or the adjacency
(e.g. someone inserts another directive between them) now fails the test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
